seoTitle: Carlingford vs Carlton — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Carlingford and Carlton. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Carlingford and Carlton.
heading: Carlingford vs Carlton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Carlingford, New South Wales and Carlton, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Carlton has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Carlingford (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Carlingford or Carlton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Carlingford has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Carlton scores 26/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Carlingford scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Carlton scores 52/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Carlingford or Carlton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Carlton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
